roleTable.title: Role Reference & Power Rules
roleTable.subtitle: Day, night abilities and how each role is assigned in the Mr. President scenario
roleTable.shieldRule: The Pope, President, Mayor, and Judge each have a protective shield that saves them from the first night kill.

pope.name: Pope
pope.dayAbility: Can veto the President's choice or the Judge's execution verdict (once per day, every other day). Cannot be put on trial by a vote.
pope.nightAbility: Has a defensive shield against shooting or terrorist actions. After every daytime veto, a random terrorist ability is added to the city at night.
pope.description: A church-linked special role that controls and overturns cabinet selections and execution decisions.

priest.name: Priest
priest.nightAbility: Each night can disable one role's ability (cannot target the same role two nights in a row).
priest.description: Responsible for temporarily blocking and disabling other roles' night abilities.

president.name: President
president.dayAbility: Once per day can swap two roles. If a cabinet seat is empty, must fill it first with a role-less player (Judge has highest priority). Cannot be put on trial by a vote.
president.nightAbility: Has a defensive shield against one shot or terrorist action.
president.description: The city's executive head with the power to reshuffle roles and keep the cabinet stable.

vice_president.name: Vice President
vice_president.dayAbility: In every court vote, his vote counts twice. If the President leaves the game, he becomes the successor.
vice_president.description: The President's right hand with double influence in court and the certain successor to the presidency.

reporter.name: Reporter
reporter.nightAbility: Point at one person and learn what happened to them during the past night.
reporter.description: A truth-seeking observer who reveals the details of nightly events for any player.

journalist.name: Journalist
journalist.nightAbility: Every other night can pick a past day or night phase and learn the true identity of anyone eliminated in that window.
journalist.description: An active journalist informed about the real identities of those killed or removed from the game.

mayor.name: Mayor
mayor.dayAbility: After the night outcomes and before the court vote, can declare a revolution and request a vote to replace the President. With a majority (half the city + 1), becomes the new President. If the vote fails, loses their role and is put on trial.
mayor.nightAbility: Has a defensive shield against shooting or terrorist actions. After every nighttime revolution, a random terrorist ability is added to the city.
mayor.description: Leader of political coups to overthrow the President and democratically seize executive power.

doctor.name: Doctor
doctor.nightAbility: Each night saves one person from a mafia/police shot. Can also save themselves each night. Protection only affects nighttime elimination.
doctor.description: A devoted physician who protects citizens' fragile lives from the guns of the night.

police.name: Police
police.nightAbility: Each night can shoot one person. After every night shot, a random terrorist ability is added to the city.
police.description: The citizens' armed power, taking nighttime shots at suspects.

detective.name: Detective
detective.nightAbility: Every other night can send one person directly to court for trial.
detective.description: A law officer who, with sufficient evidence, sends suspects to court without a vote.

judge.name: Judge
judge.dayAbility: Final verdict over court defendants (execution, prison, or pardon). Prison has 3 spots (prisoners cannot vote and are not counted, but keep their role). Cannot be put on trial by a vote.
judge.nightAbility: Has a defensive shield against shooting or terrorist actions.
judge.description: The most powerful judicial arm of the game, issuing verdicts of acquittal, execution, or imprisonment.

lawyer.name: Lawyer
lawyer.dayAbility: Before each verdict, can speak for 1 minute to defend the accused.
lawyer.description: A loyal jurist who works in court to defend the accused or sway verdicts.

none.name: No role / Plain citizen
none.dayAbility: Vote and try to identify the Freemasons.
none.description: The unaware masses pursuing the goal of saving the city from the secretive Freemasons' grip.

buttons.finish: Form cabinet and start night zero

step1.title: 1. Secret reveal of hidden identities
step1.intro: The moderator must call players one by one and, by clicking the dedicated button, privately show them their hidden identity. Click the button again to hide the card immediately.
step1.quota: The 29% quota requires exactly {{count}} freemasons. The rest are citizens.

step2.title: 1. Speaking order (first speaker)
step2.intro: At the start of day one, players take turns speaking in the day-zero discussion. You can choose the first speaker, or let the system pick randomly.
step2.guideTitle: Discussion phase and official selection guide
step2.round1Body: Speaking continues clockwise from the first speaker. Each speaker may issue one challenge (a separate speaking slot) to another player, and each player can only receive one challenge. The first speaker leads this round.
step2.round2Body: For the presidential election, each player speaks again, this time counter-clockwise starting from the same first speaker, with no challenges. In effect, the first speaker becomes the last speaker of the presidential round.
step2.candidacyBody: After the defenses end, the assembly's first vote selects the top two candidates. They then hold a short back-and-forth debate, followed by the final runoff vote.
step2.popeVetoBody: The candidate with the most votes becomes President — unless the Pope vetoes ("dislikes") them. If the Pope vetoes, the runner-up (with fewer votes) becomes the final President.

step3.title: 2. Pope election (assembly vote)
step3.desc: The player with the most votes in the day-zero assembly becomes the official Pope. Choose one player to assume the Pope's seat.

step4.title: 3. The Pope appoints a Priest
step4.desc: The elected Pope ({{pope}}) must now appoint one trusted player (other than themselves) as their personal Priest.

step5.title: 4. Final candidate vote and cabinet formation (Presidency)
step5.intro: Here the final vote among the candidates takes place. The election proceeds officially as follows:
step5.candidacyBody: In the first assembly vote, the two players with the most votes become candidates.
step5.debateBody: The two candidates defend themselves through a short verbal exchange. A final runoff vote between them follows.
step5.popeVetoBody: The candidate with the most final-round votes becomes President — unless the Pope vetoes ("dislikes") them. If the Pope vetoes the first-ranked candidate, the runner-up (with fewer votes) directly takes the presidency.
step5.finalNote: Based on the assembly vote and any Pope veto, choose the final President from the list below:
step5.vetoTitle: Pope's dislike-veto on the first-ranked President:
step5.vetoDesc: If the Pope vetoed the first-ranked candidate, enable this. Activating it spreads vengeance through the city — a random player (preferring role-less players) becomes a terrorist in upcoming night phases.
step5.vetoApply: Apply Pope veto (assign random nighttime terrorist)

step6.title: 5. Presidential cabinet formation
step6.desc: To make the government official, the President must complete the cabinet. Identify the trusted officers below and assign role-less players to each seat.

step7.descPres: The President must appoint the members under their authority — namely
step7.descVice: The Vice President (or the President if there is no Vice) must appoint the members under their command — namely

step8.title: 7. Mayor's subgroup appointment ({{name}})
step8.desc1: The active Mayor must appoint the members under their command — namely

step9.title: 8. Chief Judge's subordinate appointment ({{name}})
step9.desc1: The Chief Judge must appoint their legal arm — namely

alerts.notAllChecked: Some players haven't checked their hidden identity yet. Continue to the next step (first speaker selection)?
alerts.masonRatio:
The 29% freemason ratio is not respected!

For {{players}} players, exactly {{required}} freemasons are required.
You currently have {{current}} freemasons.

Please fix the freemason ratio in "Reveal secrets" mode.
alerts.selectSpeaker: Please pick the first speaker of the game first.
alerts.cabinetVice: Please fill all cabinet seats (Vice President, Mayor and Judge).
alerts.cabinetNoVice: Please fill all cabinet seats (Mayor and Judge).

logs.randomSpeaker: Randomly, {{name}} was chosen as the first speaker of day zero.
logs.manualMasons: The moderator manually changed the freemason lodge composition.
logs.speaker: The main day-zero speaker role was handed to {{name}}.
logs.pope: {{name}} received the highest votes and was elected as the "High Pope".
logs.priest: The High Pope appointed {{name}} as the "Priest".
logs.president: After the national vote count, {{name}} took the seat of the Presidency.
logs.reporterByVice: The Vice President appointed {{name}} as the "Reporter".
logs.journalistByVice: The Vice President appointed {{name}} as the "Journalist".
logs.doctor: The active Mayor appointed {{name}} as the "Doctor".
logs.police: The active Mayor appointed {{name}} as the "Police".
logs.detective: The active Mayor appointed {{name}} as the "Detective".
logs.lawyer: The Chief Judge appointed {{name}} as the "defense Lawyer".
logs.vetoOn: The Pope used their veto right and "disliked" the top candidate. A wave of vengeance spread through the city — a random player will become a terrorist tonight.
logs.completionWithVice: On day zero, the country was formed under a new government. President: {{president}}, Vice: {{vice}}, Mayor: {{mayor}}, Judge: {{judge}}.
logs.completionNoVice: On day zero, the country was formed under a new government. President: {{president}}, Mayor: {{mayor}}, Judge: {{judge}}.
